title: Renewable energy consumption (% of total final energy consumption)
definition: Renewable energy consumption is the share of renewables energy in total final energy consumption.

source.license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International unless specifically labeled otherwise by World Bank metadata
source.sourceOrganization: IEA Energy Statistics Data Browser, International Energy Agency (IEA), uri: https://www.iea.org/data-and-statistics/data-tools/energy-statistics-data-browser, publisher: International Energy Agency (IEA), date accessed: 2025-03-25

editorial.shortHook: A map where low-income biomass and modern renewables can both matter.
editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include Congo, Dem. Rep., Somalia, Fed. Rep., Liberia, while the lightest include Brunei Darussalam, Qatar, Algeria; that contrast is the fastest way to read the renewable energy consumption pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Renewable energy consumption forces players to separate electricity mix from total energy use.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Internet users overlaps with part of the visible pattern, but the full country spread does not match.
CO2 emissions per capita can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Basic sanitation access overlaps with part of the visible pattern, but the full country spread does not match.

editorial.difficultyReason: Expert difficulty: the map can be mistaken for adjacent indicators unless players probe a high, low, and middle country.
editorial.dataCaveat: Traditional biomass can raise this share in some lower-income countries.
regionalSignals: Energy maps often separate access, consumption, and production mix.
Look for hydro, fossil-fuel, and low-access signals.

reviewNotes: Traditional biomass caveat makes the map counterintuitive.
Expert-only until the reveal copy can carry the nuance.
